content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(i) WARN — Privacy policy states "We do not collect, process, or store your location data" but RunSessionManager.swift:1-14 uses CoreLocation (CLLocationManager, requestWhenInUseAuthorization, startUpdatingLocation) to actively track and store the user's GPS route, and description.txt:1 advertises "maps every run with GPS" — a direct contradiction.
Pierre: Apple explicitly checks that the privacy policy text matches actual data collection behavior, and this is a textbook contradiction reviewers flag manually. The app clearly collects and stores precise location data (route array) to power its core GPS-mapping feature, yet the fetched policy denies any location collection entirely. Fix the privacy policy to accurately disclose location collection, retention, and any iCloud sync/third-party use before resubmitting.
